First, the Context-Aware Knowledge Graph Construction (CKGC) module builds a unified POI knowledge graph that captures heterogeneous relationships among users, POIs, regions of interest (ROIs), and social links. Then, the Context-Aware Knowledge Graph Embedding (CKGE) module, based on the Translational Distance Model with Relation-Specific Spaces (TransR), learns relation-specific embeddings of entities to preserve heterogeneous semantics. Next, a Spatio-Temporal Gated Graph Neural Network (STG-GNN) captures temporal dynamics and spatial dependencies in user check-in behaviors, while the Relation-Aware Graph Attention Network (RA-GAT) enhances multi-relational reasoning and information aggregation across heterogeneous relations. Extensive experiments on two real-world LBSN datasets, Gowalla and Brightkite, demonstrate that CKGL significantly outperforms several baseline models on Recall and Normalized Discounted Cumulative Gain (NDCG), validating its effectiveness in capturing contextual semantics and improving recommendation accuracy under sparse and complex scenarios.

evidence.title: Personalized News Recommender System Using Hybrid Collaborative Filtering and Content-Aware Deep Models
evidence.venue: 2025 International Conference on Emerging Trends in Networks and Computer Communications (ETNCC)
evidence.abstract: In the past few years, personalised news recommender systems have gotten a lot of attention because they can give people information that is more relevant to them and makes them happier and more engaged. Traditional suggestion models depend on joint filtering, which predicts appropriate content based on how users interact with items. But these models often have problems, like not having enough data or having problems starting up after a long time. In order to get around these problems, this study suggests a mix of joint screening and content-aware deep learning models. The hybrid system takes into account both explicit and implicit user interests. It does this by using subject, mood, phrase, and interaction data from news stories along with data about how people interact with the system. A deep neural network is used to help the model better understand the complicated trends and connections between people and news material. Several performance measures, such as accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score, show that the suggested system works better than standard joint filtering models and content-based suggestion systems. It was also found that the mixed model is better at handling big numbers and is more scalable and stable. This makes it a better choice for real-world apps. This work helps personalized content delivery systems get better by solving important problems like personalization, data sparsity, and cold-start issues. It also improves the user experience by giving them more relevant and interesting news.

evidence.title: Exploring Pose-Aware Human-Object Interaction via Hybrid Learning
Collaborative filtering recommendation algorithm based on fine-grained mining and neighborhood awareness attention
Structured Pruning for Efficient Generative Pre-trained Language Models
evidence.venue: Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics
evidence.abstract: The increasing sizes of large generative Pre-trained Language Models (PLMs) hinder their deployment in real-world applications. To obtain efﬁcient PLMs, previous studies mostly focus on pruning the attention heads and feed-forward networks (FFNs) of the Transformer. Nevertheless, we ﬁnd that in generative PLMs, the hidden dimension shared by many other modules (e.g., embedding layer and layer normalization) contains persistent outliers regardless of the network input. In this study, we propose S IMPLE , a new structured pruning framework for generative PLMs that comprehensively investigates all the above compress-ible components. To identify redundant network structures, we assign learnable masks over compressible components followed by sparse training. Various sizes of PLMs can be ﬂexibly extracted via different thresholds, and are then task-speciﬁcally ﬁne-tuned for further improvement. Extensive experiments on language modeling, summarization and machine translation validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. For example, the pruned BART brings 1.51x/6.96x inference speedup on GPU/CPU with 67% size reduction, and can be further combined with quantization for more than 25 × compression.

evidence.title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
evidence.abstract: Introduces an automated scientific discovery pipeline that generates ideas, writes code, runs experiments, and drafts papers, while also exposing limits in evaluation and reliability.

evidence.title: The AI Scientist-v2: Workshop-Level Automated Scientific Discovery via Agentic Tree Search
evidence.abstract: Extends automated scientific discovery with stronger search and review-like iteration, motivating stricter gates for novelty, experiment quality, and paper readiness.

evidence.title: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
evidence.abstract: Describes agentic research assistance across literature review, experimentation, and report writing, highlighting the need for human oversight and reproducibility controls.

evidence.title: MLE-bench: Evaluating Machine Learning Agents on Machine Learning Engineering
evidence.abstract: Benchmarks agents on machine learning engineering tasks and provides a reference point for evaluating experiment-running ability and reproducibility.

evidence.title: AIBuildAI-2: Scalable Agentic AI Research and Engineering
evidence.abstract: Frames AI systems that build and improve AI systems, including multi-agent research and engineering loops with traceability requirements.

evidence.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
evidence.abstract: Uses verbal feedback and memory to help agents improve across trials, motivating explicit failure memory and revision loops.

evidence.title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
evidence.abstract: Studies iterative generation, feedback, and refinement, providing background for reviewer-style critique and revision policies.

evidence.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
evidence.abstract: Demonstrates open-ended skill acquisition with memory and iterative exploration, relevant to long-running agent loops and durable lessons.

evidence.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
evidence.abstract: Introduces agent evaluation tasks and motivates structured benchmarks for agent behavior rather than isolated text quality.

evidence.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
evidence.abstract: Shows how agents can operate computer interfaces to solve software tasks, relevant to local experiment execution and reproducible command logs.
